title: Only Authorized Local User Accounts Exist on Operating System
triage_reason: This finding involves removing local user accounts, which could potentially lock out legitimate users or break system functionality if not carefully reviewed. The rule allows customization of authorized users via a regex variable, indicating that human judgment is needed to determine which accounts are truly unauthorized.

title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Minimum Digit Characters
triage_reason: This finding modifies password complexity requirements by setting the dcredit parameter in pam_pwquality.conf. The remediation script follows standard STIG hardening patterns and only affects password policy, which is a common and safe configuration change that does not impact authentication mechanisms or system access.
attempt_1_commands: sed -i 's/^dcredit.*/dcredit = -1/' /etc/security/pwquality.conf

title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Enforce for root User
triage_reason: This finding modifies a standard PAM password policy configuration file to enforce password complexity for the root user. The remediation script follows a safe pattern: it backs up the original file, appends the enforce_for_root parameter if not present, and cleans up. This is a common STIG hardening measure that does not affect SSH access, authentication mechanisms, networking, or filesystems. The change is limited to password policy enforcement and is unlikely to cause system breakage.
attempt_1_commands: test -f /etc/security/pwquality.conf && echo "File exists" || echo "File does not exist"; grep -E "^\s*enforce_for_root" /etc/security/pwquality.conf || echo "enforce_for_root not found"; sed -i '$a enforce_for_root=1' /etc/security/pwquality.conf; echo "enforce_for_root=1" >> /etc/security/pwquality.conf; cp /etc/security/pwquality.conf /etc/security/pwquality.conf.bak; ls -la /etc/security/pwquality.conf; printf '%s\n' "enforce_for_root=1" >> /etc/security/pwquality.conf; grep -E "^\s*enforce_for_root" /etc/security/pwquality.conf; tail -1 /etc/security/pwquality.conf
attempt_2_commands: test -f /etc/security/pwquality.conf && echo "exists" || echo "does not exist"; ls -la /etc/security/pwquality.conf; cp /etc/security/pwquality.conf /etc/security/pwquality.conf.bak; cp /etc/security/pwquality.conf /etc/security/pwquality.conf.backup; grep -E "^enforce_for_root" /etc/security/pwquality.conf; sed -i '/^enforce_for_root/d' /etc/security/pwquality.conf; grep -v "^enforce_for_root" /etc/security/pwquality.conf > /tmp/pwquality_cleaned.conf; grep -E "^enforce_for_root=1" /etc/security/pwquality.conf && echo "REMEDIATION_COMPLETE" || echo "REMEDIATION_FAILED"; authselect apply-changes; grep -E "^enforce_for_root" /etc/security/pwquality.conf; echo 'enforce_for_root=1' >> /etc/security/pwquality.conf

title: Add noexec Option to /home

attempt_1_commands:

#
# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Mon Feb  9 07:40:38 2026
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk/'.
# See man pages fstab(5), findfs(8), mount(8) and/or blkid(8) for more info.
#
# After editing this file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' to update systemd
# units generated from this file.
#
/dev/mapper/rlm-root    /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=F56C-228D          /boot/efi               vfat    umask=0077,shortname=winnt 0 2
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults,noexec        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0
tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs defaults,nodev 0 0
EOF

title: Disable Kernel Parameter for IPv4 Forwarding on all IPv4 Interfaces
triage_reason: Disabling IPv4 forwarding is a standard hardening measure that prevents the system from acting as a router. The remediation involves commenting out existing sysctl entries and setting the parameter to 0, which is a safe, non-disruptive change for most systems.
attempt_1_commands: sed -i 's/^net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding.*/net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0/' /etc/sysctl.conf; echo 'net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0' > /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv4-forwarding.conf; sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding=0; sysctl net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding

title: Disable the use of user namespaces
triage_reason: Disabling user namespaces can break container functionality and may affect applications relying on them. The finding explicitly states that when containers are deployed, the value should be set to a large non-zero value, indicating this change requires careful consideration of the system's use case.
